gateRules.description: Tool GATE — carving tools (adze, drawknife, crooked knife)
gateRules.trigger: adze, drawknife, crooked knife, carving tool use
gateRules.action: GATE -- include tool safety: always adze away from body, secure the workpiece, keep adze and drawknife blades covered when not in use, seek hands-on instruction before first use of traditional carving tools

gateRules.trigger: longhouse post, beam, roof construction, structural assembly
gateRules.action: GATE -- structural safety: large cedar posts and beams require proper lifting technique and temporary support; do not work under unsupported structural members; post holes require proper depth (1/3 post length minimum) for stability

gateRules.description: Marine GATE — canoe launch and paddling
gateRules.trigger: canoe launch, paddling, water entry, reef net fishing, marine activity
gateRules.action: GATE -- marine safety required: wear PFD at all times on or near water, buddy system required (never paddle alone), know the 1-10-1 cold water rule (1 minute to control breathing, 10 minutes of meaningful movement, 1 hour before unconsciousness), check weather and tidal conditions before launch

gateRules.trigger: capsizing, swamping, falling from canoe, water immersion
gateRules.action: GATE -- cold water immersion response: 1-10-1 rule applies; do not remove clothing; stay with the vessel; use HELP position (Heat Escape Lessening Posture) to reduce heat loss; signal for assistance immediately

annotateRules.description: Tool ANNOTATE — bark harvesting tools (knife, bone wedge)
annotateRules.trigger: bark harvesting, scoring cedar bark, knife work
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- score bark vertically with sharp knife, cut away from body, keep blade covered when moving between cuts

annotateRules.trigger: bark splitting, fiber processing, inner bark tools
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- splitting tools are sharp; control direction of split by maintaining even tension; wear gloves for initial splitting steps

safetyNotes.toolSafety: Traditional cedar carving tools — adze, drawknife, crooked knife, froe — are extremely sharp and require specific body mechanics for safe use. Always adze away from the body with the workpiece firmly secured. Drawknife pulls toward the body; keep body clear of the blade path and maintain controlled pressure. Seek hands-on instruction from an experienced carver before first use. Blade guards required when tools are not in active use. Knife work for bark scoring: always cut parallel to the trunk grain and away from the body.
safetyNotes.structuralSafety: Longhouse-scale cedar construction involves members too large and heavy for solo handling. Large corner posts (20+ cm diameter, 3-5 metres long) require a team and proper lifting technique — back straight, legs driving, coordinated movement on a count. Never work under an unsupported structural member. Post holes must be at minimum one-third of the post length in depth for adequate stability. Ridge beams require temporary support structures (shoring) before permanent connections are made.
safetyNotes.marineSafety: The Salish Sea and outer Pacific coast are cold water environments year-round. Cold water shock causes an involuntary gasping reflex upon immersion — even strong swimmers can inhale water within seconds. The 1-10-1 rule applies: approximately 1 minute to control breathing, 10 minutes of meaningful movement before muscle failure, 1 hour to unconsciousness in very cold water. PFD required at all times on or near water. Never paddle alone — buddy system is non-negotiable. Check weather and tidal conditions before every launch. In capsizing: stay with the vessel, do not remove clothing, use HELP position (knees to chest, elbows tight to body) to slow heat loss.
